overview.Summary: The attack begins with a phishing campaign targeting employees of a communications company. The attackers exploit a vulnerability in the email system to gain initial access, then execute malicious code to escalate privileges and maintain persistence. They disable firewall defenses to evade detection and intercept network data to disrupt operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary sends phishing emails to employees, tricking them into clicking on malicious links. This gains initial access to the network through compromised credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: After gaining initial access, the attacker executes a remote script to install malware on the target system, enabling further attacks.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 70 Name: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: The attacker creates a new user account with basic privileges to maintain persistence and access the network at a later time.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 180 Name: Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels
stage.description: The attacker escalates privileges by exploiting misconfigured access controls, allowing full system control.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 583 Name: Disabling Network Hardware
stage.description: The attacker disables the firewall to hide malicious activities and prevent detection by security systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 158 Name: Sniffing Network Traffic
stage.description: The attacker intercepts and captures sensitive network data, leading to disruption of communications and potential data loss.

Scenario.summary: The attack starts with a phishing email that tricks employees into revealing credentials. Using these, the attacker executes malicious code, creates a persistent account, escalates privileges, disables security measures, and ultimately intercepts network data to achieve their goal of disrupting communications.
